displayTitle: Shielding by Halves, Safety by Distance

beats.narration: Two pieces of physics stand between you and an overexposure: attenuation, what matter does, and the inverse square law, what distance does.

payload.title: SHIELDING MATH
payload.sub: Predict the level before you crank the source out.

beats.narration: Shielding works in fractions: a half value layer cuts intensity in half, a tenth value layer to one tenth, and stacked layers multiply.
Run one: forty roentgens per hour must drop below five. Forty over five is eight: three halvings, so three half value layers.
Now distance: twice as far, the same rays cover four times the area, one quarter the intensity. Halve your distance and the rate quadruples.

payload.heading: The inverse square law
payload.note: HALF THE DISTANCE → FOUR TIMES THE RATE

beats.narration: One discipline ties it all together: the math predicts, the meter proves. Calculate to plan the shot, then survey after every single exposure.

payload.sub: Survey after every exposure, on every approach.
